gaps: The mechanism by which STG is recycled or dissociated from AP-3A on late endosomes remains undefined.
The identity of the adaptor/chaperone, if any, that facilitates the recycling pathway when the STG-AP-3A interaction is disrupted is unknown.
It is unclear how the relative stoichiometry of AP-2 versus AP-3A competition for STG binding is regulated in the short time windows (3-10 min) post-NMDA.
questions_for_expert: Is the 'sequential' binding of STG to AP-2 and then AP-3A strictly dependent on the differential affinity of the dephosphorylated STG for these adaptors, or is it driven by the physical maturation of the endosomal compartment?
Does the 'high-mobility' form of STG observed in brain lysates correspond strictly to the dephosphorylated state, or is there an intermediate species not captured by current blotting?
